data.title: Wall Street pushes higher on Black Friday
data.description:
Wall Street pushed higher on Friday after a disruption at CME's (CME) data centers temporarily halted trading in many markets, including the stock index futures.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was last higher by 0.3%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) climbed up by 0.2% and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) advanced by 0.2%. 

Looking at the S&P 500 by sector, ten of the 11 segments are in positive territory with Energy leading the way. At the other end of the spectrum, Health Care was the weakest performing area. 

Treasury yields ticked higher as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up by 2 basis points to 4.01% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.48%. 

The economic calendar is light, with mainly the Fed balance sheet slated to land later in the day. 

As for stocks that were are on the move, shares of Intel (INTC) were higher by 5% while shares of Eli Lilly (LLY) fell 2.5%. 

Moreover, U.S. markets are coming off the Thanksgiving holiday and will operate on a shorter schedule today, closing at 1 p.m. ET for Black Friday.

data.title: Bitcoin extends rebound from last week's selloff, crypto-tied stocks climb
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) added to its recent recovery after a multi-week selloff, amid broader gains across risk assets, including crypto-tied equities.

The price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) drifted up 1% to $92.2K in the last 24 hours as of 10:14 a.m. ET, after briefly testing the $93K level earlier in the session. BTC was climbing in the run-up to the Thanksgiving holiday -- rebounding from the recent ~$80K trough -- in a move partly backed by net inflows into spot crypto exchange-traded funds. Still, BTC is ~19% lower from a month earlier.

Ether (ETH-USD), the world's largest altcoin by market cap, advanced 1.6% to $3.06K at press time. Other tokens on the move include: Hyperliquid (HYPE-USD) +4.8%, Mantle (MANTLE-USD) +2.8%, Uniswap (UNI-USD) +2.4%, Sui (SUI-USD) +2.3% and Aster (ASTER-USD) +2.3%.

Crypto funds: iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) +2.7%, Grayscale Bitcoin Trust (GBTC) +2.7%, CoinShares Bitcoin and Ether ETF (BTF) +1.8%, Grayscale Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHE) +1.4%, Grayscale Solana Trust (GSOL) -0.5%.

Crypto-tied stocks: Strategy (MSTR) +3.8%, Coinbase Global (COIN) +3.9%, MARA Holdings (MARA) +5.5%, Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) +7.4%, Bakkt Holdings (BKKT) +6%, SharpLink Gaming (SBET) +4.1%, Gemini Space Station (GEMI) +2%, Bullish (BLSH) +1.7%, Figure Technology Solutions (FIGR) +1.4%.

At the same time, the U.S. stock market edged higher in Friday's shortened session after a widespread disruption at one of CME Group's (CME) data centers disrupted many markets. Crypto markets always stay open.

data.title: BofA’s ‘triple momentum’ stocks in gold, defense, nuclear, AI: WDC, GD, NVDA, and more
data.description:
Gold stocks (GLD), defense (ITA), (XAR), nuclear (NLR), (NUKZ), and rare earths (REMX) subsectors rank above all global sectors, according to Bank of America’s “triple momentum” analysis.

The analysis, which combines earnings, price, and news momentum indicators, shows active long-only funds maintaining positions close to benchmark weight in these sectors.

The assessment combines these characteristics, stock rankings, investor positioning, and valuation trends to provide a complete picture of current market sentiment. This approach aims to determine “how much has been priced in” for each sector, according to BofA, offering investors guidance on where opportunities might still exist despite the strong performance already observed in these thematic investments.

 

 

 

Within each investment theme, BofA Quant strategists identified top-performing stocks across different regions.

In the U.S., Celestica (CLS), Seagate Technology (STX), Western Digital (WDC), Lumentum Holdings (LITE), and Amkor Technology (AMKR) are the stocks with the highest triple momentum ranking within the AI theme.

Within the defense theme, the highest-ranking stocks are IonQ (IONQ), V2X (VVX), General Dynamics (GD), BWX Technologies (BWXT), and Leidos (LDOS).

Within gold: New Gold (NGD), Equinox Gold (EQX), IAMGOLD Corp. (IAG), Barrick Mining (B), and Dundee Precious (OTCPK:DPMLF).

Within nuclear energy: Talen Energy (TLN) and BWX Technologies (BWXT).

Within quantum computing: Quantum Computing (QUBT), IonQ (IONQ), and Nvidia (NVDA).

Lastly, within rare earths, the group is headed by MP Materials (MP) in the U.S.

Investment positioning data also revealed a notable underweight stance by investors in quantum computing and AI sectors despite their strong momentum. BofA strategists explained this phenomenon is “largely because of the difficulty overweighting stocks with extremely large index weights,” while noting that funds have been actively buying shares across all themes, with quantum computing (QTUM) seeing the most significant additions over the past year.

In addition, valuation metrics showed substantial variation across these sectors, with implications for potential future performance, said Nigel Tupper, chief global quantitative strategist at BofA.

According to the analysis, “rare earths (REMX) have the highest prospective PE, while gold (GLD) has the lowest,” providing insight into relative valuation levels.

BofA strategists further observed that “quantum computing (QTUM) has the highest price-to-book trailing while nuclear (NLR), (NUKZ) has the lowest,” suggesting different risk-reward profiles across these investment themes.

data.title: Canada invests $210 million in IBM Bromont and C2MI to boost semiconductor packaging capacity
data.description:
Canada is investing up to $210 million through the Strategic Response Fund to back a $662 million initiative that will expand semiconductor packaging and commercialization capacity at IBM Canada’s Bromont site and the MiQro Innovation Collaborative Centre (C2MI).

The investment comes as Canada looks to strengthen domestic production of semiconductors, including AI and high-performance computing components that will drive the competitiveness and resilience of Canada’s AI ecosystem.

The project is expected to create significant growth in the province of Quebec, building on earlier phases to create 75 new highly skilled jobs and maintain more than a thousand jobs in the Bromont region.

"The collaboration with IBM and C2MI will significantly enhance Canada’s manufacturing prowess and support capabilities for next-generation semiconductors,” François-Philippe Champagne, Minister of Finance and National Revenue, said.

"The government’s continued investments to develop the semiconductor industry will help ensure a strong, predictable supply of the semiconductors needed by Canadian businesses and consumers."

data.title: Anglo's proposed Teck takeover clears Canada’s national security test - Globe & Mail
data.description:
Canada's government has cleared Anglo American's (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) proposed takeover of Teck Resources (TECK) on national security grounds, The Globe and Mail reported Thursday, removing a key hurdle for the deal to close.

The government’s initial security review period for assessing whether proposed foreign takeovers of Canadian critical minerals companies has lapsed without an extension, meaning the deal has been cleared by default on national security grounds, according to the report.

The government still has the power to block the takeover if it fails to clear a net economic benefit review; Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) has said that getting through all the red tape to complete the deal could take as long as 18 months.

Shareholders of Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) and Teck (TECK) are scheduled to vote on the deal December 9 at meetings in London and Vancouver; shareholder proxy advisory firms Glass Lewis and Institutional Shareholder Services have endorsed the transaction.

data.title: CME markets now open and trading, Globex FX Spot+ markets to reportedly pre-open at 08:30 central time
data.description:
CME Group (CME) said in a social media post that its markets are now open and trading after a cooling issue at CyrusOne data centers led to a halt in trading across its markets.

The derivatives exchange operator said in an X post, "All CME Group markets are open and trading."

Also, CME Globex FX Spot+ markets will pre-open at 08:30 AM central time, Bloomberg News reported separately.

data.title: Oracle, Vantage in talks with lenders for $38B in debt to fund data centers: report
data.description:
• A consortium of banks and lenders are in discussions with Oracle (ORCL) and Vantage Data Centers to lend the pair an additional $38B to build data centers for OpenAI (OPENAI), the Financial Times reported.
  • The deal is expected to be finalized in the next several weeks, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. 
  • Oracle shares fell 3% in premarket trading on Friday, as concerns about its debt load have continued to rise in recent weeks.
  • Oracle did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Ryanair pulls the plug on Prime membership as low turnout makes program too costly
data.description:
Ryanair (RYAAY) is ending its Ryanair Prime program saying the €79 ($91.40) membership fee and low turnout failed to earn enough money to make the program financially viable.

After an 8-month trial, the Irish discount airline said that as of November 28 it will not sign any new members and existing members will have until October 2026 to enjoy exclusive discounted fares.

With just 55,000 members – far short of the 250,000 goal – the program cost more money than the €4.4M in subscription fees it generated.

“Our Prime members have received over €6m in fare discounts, so this trial has cost more money than it generates. This level of memberships, or subscription revenue does not justify the time and effort it takes to launch monthly exclusive Prime seat sales,” Ryanair chief marketing officer Dara Brady said.

Although the company never had ambitious expectations for Prime, the cost outweighed its usefulness.

“It’s never going to be huge,” CEO Michael Leary said on the company’s fiscal fourth quarter earnings call in May, referring to the expected turnout for Prime.

“We’re probably at this point in time going to generate about €2.5M in membership fees in a company where we’re looking at making €1.6B is not huge. But I think it is very critical in our ability to target seat sales,” Leary added, conceding that the carrier should have priced Prime at €99 to justify the cost savings for members.

Ryanair (RYAAY) shares are down as much as 6% in early Friday trading, putting the stock on track snap a four-day winning streak that elevated RYAAY to an all-time high.

data.title: Rubico announces reverse stock split
data.description:
• Rubico (NASDAQ:RUBI)  announced on Friday that it has determined to effect a 1-for-30 reverse stock split of the company’s issued common shares, par value $0.01, effective at the opening of trading on December 2, 2025.
  • The company had 61.44M outstanding shares, which will be reduced to approximately 2.05M, to adjust cancellation of fractional shares as of November 20, 2025.
  • The split will not have any effect on shareholders's ownership percentage of common shares, market capitalization of the company, or on any voting rights.
  • Fractional shares will be settled in cash.
  • The purpose of the reverse stock split is to increase the market price and maintain compliance with the Nasdaq's listing requirements.
  • Shares -8.20%.

data.title: Google drops Microsoft antitrust complaint in EU after new investigation
data.description:
Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) said on Friday that it dropped its antitrust complaint against Microsoft (MSFT) in the European Union over concerns about its cloud computing practices after regulators launched a new investigation against the Windows maker.

Google filed an antitrust complaint with the European Commission against Microsoft in September 2024, accusing it of unfair licensing practices regarding its Azure cloud computing service.

Earlier this month, the E.C. opened investigations into Microsoft's and Amazon's (AMZN) dominance in cloud computing under the Digital Markets Act.

The first two probes will assess whether Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure should be classified as “gatekeepers” for their cloud computing services, and whether they act as important gateways between businesses and consumers. The inquiry comes even as the two services do not meet the DMA gatekeeper thresholds for size, user number and market position. 

A third investigation will explore whether the DMA is “effective in addressing practices that limit competitiveness or are unfair in the cloud sector.”

Together, Amazon, Microsoft, and Google hold roughly 70% of the European cloud computing market, according to Synergy Research. 

“The investigation will cover, for instance, obstacles to interoperability between cloud computing services, limited or conditioned access for business users to data, tying and bundling services, and potentially imbalanced contractual terms,” according to the commission’s statement.

The commission plans to conclude its probe in 12 months and publish a final report within 18 months. If Amazon (AMZN) or Microsoft (MSFT) are found to be gatekeeping their cloud computing services, they will have six months to ensure compliance with DMA rules.

(Seeking Alpha's Preeti Singh contributed to this story.)
